front: What is the definition of a stationary time series?
back: A time series is stationary if its statistical properties such as mean, variance, and autocorrelation are constant over time. This means there is no trend or seasonal effect, and the series fluctuates around a constant mean with constant variance.

front: What is the general form of an Autoregressive (AR) model of order p?
back: An AR(p) model is defined as: X_t = c + φ₁X_{t-1} + φ₂X_{t-2} + ... + φ_pX_{t-p} + ε_t, where c is a constant, φ₁,...,φ_p are the parameters of the model, and ε_t is white noise.

front: What is the general form of a Moving Average (MA) model of order q?
back: An MA(q) model is defined as: X_t = μ + ε_t + θ₁ε_{t-1} + θ₂ε_{t-2} + ... + θ_qε_{t-q}, where μ is the mean of the series, ε_t is white noise, and θ₁,...,θ_q are the parameters of the model.

front: What are the defining characteristics of white noise, denoted as ε_t?
back: White noise is a sequence of uncorrelated random variables with a mean of zero and a constant finite variance, σ². Formally: E[ε_t] = 0, Var(ε_t) = σ², and Cov(ε_t, ε_s) = 0 for all t ≠ s.

front: What is an Autoregressive Moving Average (ARMA) model?
back: An ARMA(p, q) model combines both Autoregressive (AR) and Moving Average (MA) components. It is defined as: X_t = c + φ₁X_{t-1} + ... + φ_pX_{t-p} + ε_t + θ₁ε_{t-1} + ... + θ_qε_{t-q}, where {φ} are the AR parameters and {θ} are the MA parameters.
